etymology.description: semantic 力 (力) (lì, "strong arm") + phonetic 且 (且) (qiě) -> to help with one’s strength
etymology.interpretation: The character 助 (zhù, "aid") combines 力 (力) (lì, "strong arm") as the semantic element, suggesting the use of strength, with 且 (且) (qiě) as the phonetic element providing the sound. The concept links physical strength to the act of assisting, thus forming the meaning of 'aid' or 'support.'

examples.chinese: 他总是乐于助人(他總是樂於助人)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì lè yú zhù rén.
examples.english: He is always willing to help others.

examples.chinese: 需要帮助的时候(需要幫助的時候)，可以随时找我(可以隨時找我)。
examples.pinyin: Xūyào bāngzhù de shíhòu, kěyǐ suíshí zhǎo wǒ.
examples.english: When you need help, you can reach out to me anytime.

examples.chinese: 他的成功离不开家人的支持和帮助(他的成功離不開家人的支持和幫助)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de chénggōng lí bù kāi jiārén de zhīchí hé bāngzhù.
examples.english: His success is inseparable from the support and help of his family.
